project.name: rust_ai_sdk tool-aware chat evolution
project.goal: Extend the SDK from text-only generate/stream primitives into a provider-neutral, tool-aware chat SDK suitable for a Rust chat server behind a static Vite frontend.
non_goals: Do not build the outer HTTP chat server in this repo
Do not add database persistence in this repo
Do not implement browser transport protocol adapters in this repo
Do not add resumable streams in this repo
Do not make MCP the first-class design target for v1

confirmed_from_repo: TextRequest currently contains messages, max_output_tokens, and temperature
Message currently contains role plus plain string content
StreamEvent currently contains TextDelta and Finished
TextResult returns final text plus finish_reason, usage, and response metadata
OpenAI provider supports generate and streaming already
OpenAI examples for generate and stream already exist

artifacts_required.description: Detailed natural-language implementation prompt with context, goals, non-goals, required capabilities, examples, and acceptance criteria.
Machine-readable stateful task list for continuing implementation.

tasks.title: Introduce structured message content model
tasks.description: Replace plain string message content with structured message parts that can represent text, tool calls, and tool results.
deliverables: Migration helpers for simple text-only messages
acceptance_criteria: A message can represent plain text, tool calls, and tool results
Simple user/system/assistant text messages remain ergonomic to construct
Tests cover basic construction and serialization assumptions if serialization is present

tasks.title: Extend TextRequest with tool metadata
tasks.description: Add provider-neutral tool definitions and optional tool choice policy to the request model.
acceptance_criteria: A request can carry zero or more tool definitions
The core type does not depend on application callback functions
Existing text-only use remains straightforward

tasks.title: Expand StreamEvent for tool-aware streaming
tasks.description: Add stream events that can represent tool-call lifecycle information in addition to text deltas and completion.
deliverables: Tests for mixed text and tool-call event sequences
acceptance_criteria: A caller can distinguish text generation from tool activity
The event model is provider-neutral
The final completion event still carries finish reason, usage, and response metadata

tasks.title: Add richer final chat result types
tasks.description: Add a structured final result type that can return an assistant message in structured form rather than plain text only.
deliverables: Decision on retaining TextResult as convenience or compatibility surface
acceptance_criteria: Final assistant output can contain structured message parts
Finish reason, usage, and response metadata remain available
Text-only consumers still have a clear path

tasks.title: Add runtime helper for one model turn with tool detection
tasks.description: Create a higher-level runtime helper that runs one model turn and returns either a completed response or a structured tool-request outcome.
deliverables: Outcome type for completed vs tool-requested turn
Unit tests for text-only and tool-request outcomes
acceptance_criteria: A caller can run one turn and know whether tools need execution
The runtime does not own application tool policy
The API is simple enough for outer server use

tasks.title: Add runtime helper for model-tool-model continuation flow
tasks.description: Provide a reusable helper or reference runtime pattern for continuing after tool execution.
deliverables: Tests for multi-step model → tool → model flow
acceptance_criteria: An application can append tool results and continue cleanly
The runtime remains application-owned for actual tool execution
The behavior is covered by tests or deterministic examples

tasks.title: Implement OpenAI request translation for tools
tasks.description: Update the OpenAI adapter so it can translate tool-enabled requests and structured responses.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI requests can include tools
OpenAI responses map into structured assistant messages
Provider-specific tests validate the mapping

tasks.title: Implement OpenAI streaming translation for tool-aware events
tasks.description: Update the OpenAI streaming adapter to emit structured tool-aware events while preserving existing finish handling and metadata behavior.
deliverables: Mocked streaming tests with tool-related payloads
acceptance_criteria: Text and tool activity are surfaced as distinct stream events
Finish, usage, and metadata carry-forward continue to work
Error handling remains clear and tested

tasks.title: Create example: text-only generate
tasks.description: Add a runnable example demonstrating the new API for a simple non-tool generate flow.
acceptance_criteria: The example runs
The API remains ergonomic for simple use
The example does not require tool support

tasks.title: Create example: text-only stream
tasks.description: Add a runnable example demonstrating streaming without tools using the new API.
acceptance_criteria: The example runs
Streaming remains ergonomic for text-only use
The example makes the event flow obvious

tasks.title: Create example: tool-aware non-streaming turn
tasks.description: Add a runnable example where a model requests a simple deterministic tool and the application feeds the result back.
acceptance_criteria: The example clearly demonstrates the tool request and continuation flow
The tool is deterministic and easy to understand
The example validates API ergonomics

tasks.title: Create example: tool-aware streaming turn
tasks.description: Add a runnable example that mirrors the actual target architecture: stream text, detect tool request, execute tool externally, continue, and stream final answer.
acceptance_criteria: The example is runnable
The flow resembles the intended Rust chat server architecture
A reader can understand how to wire the outer app after reading it

tasks.title: Create example: event inspection
tasks.description: Add a dedicated example that prints structured stream events for inspection and ergonomics review.
acceptance_criteria: The event model is easy to inspect from the example output
The example helps validate stream API design

tasks.title: Document example execution and expected behavior
tasks.description: Add minimal docs so the new examples are runnable and their intent is obvious.
acceptance_criteria: A reader can run each example without guesswork
Tool examples clearly state what is mocked or deterministic

tasks.title: Extend second provider after core design stabilizes
tasks.description: Apply the same abstractions to Anthropic or the second provider path once the core and OpenAI implementation are stable.
acceptance_criteria: The second provider can participate in the same core abstractions
The core is not OpenAI-specific
